location: Found inside Fringefolk Hero's Grave, a hidden dungeon behind a fog gate at the back of the Stranded Graveyard (the tutorial death location) in Limgrave — the gate needs 2 Stonesword Keys to open. The bow itself is claimed by destroying the dungeon's runaway Chariot trap rather than picking it off the ground.
tactics: Unlock the fog gate at the back of Stranded Graveyard with 2 Stonesword Keys, then head in avoiding the runaway Chariot (it one- or two-shots low-level characters) until the path narrows and you can drop to its side.

Past the imps and a fire trap you reach a room with an altar guarded by two ambushing Grafted Scions that drop from the ceiling — that room holds a different item (the Erdtree's Favor talisman), so don't mistake it for your goal; drop down the ledge instead of crossing the bridge to bypass the ambush entirely.

Take the elevator up, go up the next stairs and turn left (watch for a Dragon Knight Shade nearby), then shoot the rope holding a cluster of suspended pots so they crash onto the Chariot as it rolls past, destroying it and dropping the Erdtree Greatbow.
lore: Styled after the Erdtree itself, this bow was made in an age when battle and faith were one and the same, designed as a companion piece to the Golden Great Arrow. It channels the wielder's Faith into every shot, hitting hardest when loaded with holy-infused arrows.

builds.name: Faith Archer
builds.why: D-scaling Faith dwarfs its E-scaling Strength/Dexterity, and it deals 20% bonus damage firing Golden Great Arrows, giving a Faith/incantation build a rare long-range option.

builds.name: Str/Fai hybrid
builds.why: Modest 20 Strength / 14 Dexterity requirements let a Faith-leaning melee character carry it as a sniping option without reworking their build.
